articles.title: ₹42-cr. fine slapped for stone quarry violation
articles.description: The Department of Mines has slapped a quarry operator with a hefty fine of ₹42 crore for quarrying 40,000 cubic metres of stones in excess of the permissible limit. articles.title: Mumbai’s Biggest Traffic Experiment Begins
articles.description: Mumbai’s Bandra-Kurla Complex (BKC) will observe a ‘Public Transport Day’ every Friday starting June 12, as part of a new initiative to reduce traffic congestion and encourage the use of public transport. The move has been introduced by MMRDA in collaboration with transport authorities and major offices in the business district. Employees working in BKC are being encouraged to avoid using private cars and two-wheelers and instead travel by metro, local trains, buses, or shared transport. Authorities say nearly two lakh people commute to BKC daily, and the initiative aims to ease traffic pressure, improve mobility, and reduce vehicle emissions in the area.

articles.title: 308% return on SGB premature redemption date: Gold bond turns Rs 1 lakh investment into Rs 4.08 lakh
articles.description: The RBI has announced the premature redemption price for Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) 2019-20 Series-VII, set at Rs 15,275 per unit. Investors can redeem these bonds from June 10, 2026, after the fifth year of issuance. This offers a significant return of approximately 308% on the initial investment.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO: Listing date, valuation among 10 things to know about the biggest stock market debut ever
articles.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is poised for a historic IPO, aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation. Investor demand is strong, with shares set to list on Nasdaq on June 12. The offering prioritizes retail investors and will see Elon Musk retain significant control, despite the company currently operating at a loss.
